title: AgentHandoffArtifact
description: Standard schema for inter-agent handoff artifacts in multi-agent workflows. Every artifact passed between agents must conform to this schema. See templates/artifact-schema.json and .github/skills/multi-agent-planner.yml.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the artifact was created.

source_agent.description: Skill name of the agent that produced this artifact (e.g. multi-agent-planner).

target_agent.description: Skill name of the agent that should consume this artifact (e.g. executor).

workflow_run_id.description: GitHub Actions workflow run ID for the run that produced this artifact.

task_id.description: Unique identifier for the task or sub-task this artifact represents (e.g. ST-002).

status.description: Current status of the task associated with this artifact.

requires_human_approval.description: Always true for Level 2+ handoffs. The receiving agent must not execute until a human has approved.

approved_by.description: GitHub username of the human who approved execution. Required when status is approved or completed.

payload.description: Task-specific data passed from source to target agent.

inputs.description: Key-value map of inputs required by the target agent.

expected_outputs.description: List of output items the target agent must produce.

path.description: File path or artifact name, if applicable.

stop_conditions.description: Conditions that must cause the target agent to halt and request human review.

permissions.description: GitHub Actions permissions required by the target agent for this task.

audit_trail.description: Links to evidence that this handoff occurred.

payload.description: Implement: add farewell() function to src/greet.py with a unit test.
